Description for Majors: Application of engineering to biology and medicine, integrating molecular biology with quantitative and computational tools.
Broad study of living systems from molecules and cells to organisms and populations, with strong research integration.
Interdisciplinary option combining neuroscience, computer science, and engineering to study how brains and intelligent systems compute.
Study of the nervous system across molecular, cellular, systems, and behavioral levels.
Engineering of chemical and biomolecular processes, including reaction engineering, transport phenomena, and materials.
Study of matter and its transformations across organic, inorganic, physical, and analytical chemistry, with substantial research participation.
Interdisciplinary option in environmental systems with a chemistry emphasis, administered through CCE.
Mathematics applied to scientific and engineering problems with strong training in numerical methods, modeling, and computation.
Study of physics with an engineering orientation including optics, condensed matter, plasmas, and quantum devices.
Quantitative major combining economics, finance, accounting, and management science.
Foundations and applications of computing including algorithms, systems, theory, AI, and software.
Study of circuits, signals, communications, control, electromagnetics, semiconductor devices, and electronic systems.
Customizable engineering option allowing students to design an interdisciplinary engineering program in consultation with faculty.
Option focused on the mathematical, statistical, and computational foundations of data science and machine learning.
Study of the structure, properties, and processing of metals, ceramics, polymers, semiconductors, and nanomaterials.
Study of mechanics, thermodynamics, fluids, materials, design, robotics, and aerospace systems.
Engineering option focused on the mechanics of solids and structures, including elasticity, plasticity, and failure analysis.
Quantitative study of stars, galaxies, cosmology, and high-energy astrophysical phenomena (offered through the GPS division alongside PMA).
Interdisciplinary option in environmental systems with a physics emphasis, administered through GPS.
Study of Earth's and other planets' physical and chemical processes, geology, geophysics, and planetary science.
Study of how individuals, firms, and societies allocate resources, with strong quantitative and game-theoretic emphasis.
Study of literatures in English with attention to form, history, and theory.
Study of the human past across periods, regions, and themes.
Interdisciplinary option examining the history and philosophy of scientific thought and practice.
Study of fundamental questions in metaphysics, epistemology, ethics, logic, and the philosophy of science.
Study of political institutions, behavior, theory, and formal political analysis, with strong quantitative training.
Interdisciplinary option in visual studies, art history, and the cultural analysis of images and media.
Pure mathematics including analysis, algebra, geometry, topology, and number theory; one of the most research-intensive undergraduate math programs in the U.S.
Rigorous study of classical mechanics, electromagnetism, quantum mechanics, statistical mechanics, and contemporary physics, with significant research integration.
